AMD.reasoning:
Branch A fires on statistical extremes but lacks reversal-candle confirmation, reducing conviction. Branch B does not apply (price at swing highs, no Fibonacci pullback).

Branch A — Fade the Extreme: Daily Z-score(50) = 2.6 (above +2 threshold). RSI-14 = 83.06, RSI-21 = 86.46 — deep overbought, well above the >75 fade zone. Distance from 50-SMA = +52.64% — extreme extension (>10% threshold). Bollinger %B = 0.929, near but not yet outside the upper band (upper at 367.1 vs price 354.49). Daily ADX = 74.94 with strong +DI dominance (55.37 vs 10.1) — the trend is brutally strong, which argues against a quick fade.

Hourly context: RSI-21 = 66.57 (elevated but not extreme); hourly Z-score(120) = 1.74 (below the +2 threshold). Hourly ADX = 17.25 (weak) and hourly MACD histogram near zero (0.084 with bullish crossover still intact). The hourly frame is NOT confirming exhaustion alongside the daily extreme — this divergence between daily and hourly extremes is a caution flag against entering the fade now.

No RSI divergence detected on either timeframe. The Apr 30 candle (open 341.36, close 354.49, high 354.96) closed near its high on slightly above-average volume (1.05x) — a bullish continuation candle, NOT a reversal candle. The strategy requires a reversal candle before entry.

Recent price context: AMD dipped sharply to 310 on Apr 28, then recovered +14% in 2 days to close at 354.49 near all-time recent highs. This V-recovery with expanding volume confirms momentum remains intact short-term.

Wiki context (Apr 17 run): prior RSI was 91 with bearish hourly STC cross — already identified as extreme overbought. Current RSI of 83 is lower, but the overbought condition has persisted across multiple runs. Repeated overbought reads without a clean reversion lower conviction for a fresh fade.

Conclusion: Branch A setup is valid in principle (Z-score 2.6, RSI 83) but the entry condition (reversal candle) has not been met. Signal is bearish with moderate-low confidence. A confirming bearish candle or gap-down open near 350+ would upgrade confidence to 65-70. Entry on anticipated reversal from extreme; target is near-term hourly support / mean reversion zone.
